nodes.title: Engineer 3 Disengagement
nodes.subtitle: Key engineer showing burnout signals
nodes.description: Engineer 3 has shown a 72% drop in commit frequency over the past 3 weeks, combined with declining Slack activity and missed standups. Pattern matches historical attrition signals.
nodes.evidence: Commit frequency dropped 72%. Slack messages down 58%. Missed 4 standups in 2 weeks. Sentiment score: 0.23 (critical).

nodes.title: Deploy Velocity Dropped 60%
nodes.subtitle: CI/CD pipeline throughput declining
nodes.description: Deployment frequency has fallen from 12/week to 5/week, correlating with Engineer 3's reduced output and growing tech debt in the build system.
nodes.evidence: Deploy frequency: 5/week (was 12). Build times up 40%. 3 rollbacks in past week. Pipeline queue depth: 2.3x normal.

nodes.title: DM Feature Broken
nodes.subtitle: Critical messaging feature degraded
nodes.description: The direct messaging feature—owned primarily by Engineer 3—has accumulated 14 unresolved bugs and 3 critical performance regressions since disengagement began.
nodes.evidence: 14 open bugs in DM module. P0 latency regression: 340ms → 1.2s. Feature adoption dropped 23%. 47 support tickets this week.

nodes.title: CI Failure Rate Spike
nodes.subtitle: Build reliability deteriorating
nodes.description: CI failure rate has climbed to 34%, up from a baseline of 8%. Flaky tests and untested merges from reduced code review coverage are primary drivers.
nodes.evidence: CI failure rate: 34% (baseline 8%). Flaky test count: 23. Average retry count: 2.1. Developer wait time: +45min/day.

nodes.title: SLA Contract At Risk
nodes.subtitle: Uptime guarantees threatened
nodes.description: Current infrastructure degradation trajectory puts 3 enterprise SLA contracts at risk of breach within 30 days, potentially triggering penalty clauses.
nodes.evidence: 3 enterprise SLAs with 99.9% uptime clause. Current uptime: 99.2%. Penalty exposure: $340K. Contract renewal in 45 days.

nodes.title: Churn Rate Spike +6.4%
nodes.subtitle: User retention declining sharply
nodes.description: Monthly churn has increased from 3.2% to 9.6%, driven by DM feature degradation and growing user frustration with product reliability.
nodes.evidence: Monthly churn: 9.6% (was 3.2%). NPS dropped 18 points. 'Cancel' page visits up 340%. Competitor mentions in churn surveys: 67%.

nodes.title: Revenue Cliff Risk
nodes.subtitle: MRR erosion accelerating
nodes.description: Combined impact of churn acceleration and SLA penalties creates a projected 22% MRR decline over 60 days, threatening runway assumptions.
nodes.evidence: Projected MRR decline: 22% over 60 days. Current MRR: $1.2M. At-risk ARR: $3.1M. Runway impact: -2.3 months.
